rulings.Case Title: CHRISTY CAMARA GOMES VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 1. 1 - PETITIONER CHRISTY GOMES' Motion And Motion For Judgment. Petitioner's motion for judgment on her petition for writ of mandate under the California Public Records Act ("CPRA") is granted except for documents 9 through 14 as identified in exhibit 8 to the Alioto declaration. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTY CAMARA GOMES VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Seal Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 1. 2 - RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE's MOTION To Seal Records. Respondent's motion to seal is denied. Respondent fails to show that an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access warrants sealing the records. (CRC 2.550(d).) This is true even if the adduced documents, which may be publicly available from other sources, come from a confidential database or are transmitted on a confidential communication system. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: REGINA PACHECO VS. MINDFUL VENTURE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANT ARI QAYUMI's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Ari Qayumi's motion to set aside default and default judgment is denied. Qayumi moves under CCP 473(b) and argues she committed excusable neglect in failing to realize her former attorney was not defending her in this matter. Such relief can be granted no later than six months after entry of judgment. Here, default judgement was entered on October 2, 2023. The six-month deadline to seek 473(b) relief expired on April 2, 2024. Qayumi filed this motion on September 5, 2024, five months too late. Qayumi also moves under CCP 418.10(a)(1) and argues service of summons should be set aside. Such relief must be sought on or before the last day to plead, or within any further time that the court may for good cause allow. Here, Qayumi was personally served on May 18, 2023, so she had until mid-June 2023 to answer the complaint. (CCP 412.20(a)(3).) Her motion is thus untimely by over a year, and there is no good cause to allow it at this late stage. Qayumi argues that plaintiff Regina Pacheco's recently filed motion for assignment of rights requires a response that somehow renders this motion timely. No authority for this proposition is provided. Post-judgment collections efforts do not re-open time to plead or otherwise make this CCP 418.10 motion timely. Although a reference to CCP 473(d) was made in Qayumi's notice of motion, her memorandum does not set forth any argument as to why relief is warranted under this provision. The court does not reach this issue. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: REGINA PACHECO VS. MINDFUL VENTURE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights And Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 7. 2 - PLAINTIFF REGINA PACHECO's Motion For Assignment Of Rights And Restraining Order. Plaintiff Regina Pacheco's motion for assignment of rights and restraining order is granted. Defendant Ari Qayumi does not address the assignment issue directly. Rather, she repeats arguments from her motion to set aside default and default judgement as to why the underlying judgment is void. That motion is denied, and Qayumi raises no arguments for this court to consider regarding this assignment. Pacheco is thus entitled to an assignment of rights to satisfy the $205,843 default judgment against Qayumi and Mindful Venture Capital Management, LLC. All money in the account owned by Judgment Debtor Ari Qayumi and/or Judgment Debtor Mindful Ventures Capital Management, LLC at J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, NA ending in account number 5530 is assigned to Judgment Creditor Regina Pacheco or the San Francisco Sheriff for the benefit of Judgment Creditor Regina Pacheco, pursuant to the provisions of C.C.P. section 708.510 et seq. Judgment Debtor Ari Qayumi or Judgment Debtor Mindful Ventures Capital Management, LLC are restrained from assigning or otherwise disposing of any rights to the funds in the account at J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, NA ending in account number 5530 which is owned by Judgment Debtor Ari Qayumi and/or Judgment Debtor Mindful Ventures Capital Management, LLC. J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, NA or any of its subsidiaries shall transfer the money in that account and/or direct Judgment Debtor Ari Qayumi or Judgment Debtor Mindful Ventures Capital Management, LLC to direct J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, NA to transfer the money in that account. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Put A Stop To Discrimination Happening In This Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 19. 3 - PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI's Motion To Put A Stop To Discrimination Happening In This Litigation. Off calendar. Not timely served and filed per CCP 1005. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 13. DEFENDANT ENLIGHTENED MANAGEMENT COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. CYCLE GEAR, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 15. PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff's unopposed motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SETH WHITAKER VS. SERVICENOW, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Ratifying Withdrawal From Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, September 30, 2024, Line 18. 3 - PLAINTIFF SETH WHITAKER's Motion For Order Ratifying Withdrawal From Arbitration. Plaintiff Seth Whitaker's (Whitaker) motion for an order ratifying withdrawal from arbitration is granted. Defendant ServiceNow, Inc.'s (ServiceNow) motion to compel arbitration is denied. The court grants Whitaker's motion for the same reasons it denies ServiceNow's motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
